parties.name: MOLLOY, ISABEL 759 33RD AVENUE SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94121

calendar.matters:
PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
Off Calendar Jul-31-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Isabel Molloy in Propria Persona.
Selinda Neal (Daughter of Isabel) also present.

The Court noted that Ms. Molloy did everything that they talked about at the last hearing, that the County Clerk's Office has no record of a marriage license and that the Church has no specifics as to a marriage license. Ms. Molloy stated that there is a Church record, but no legal form. The Petition to Establish Fact of Marriage is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
Off Calendar Jul-15-2019 Continued To Jul-29-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Isabel Molloy in Propria Persona.
Daughter also present.

The Court stated that what is needed to establish fact of marriage is a license that was issued from the County, not returned to the County. Ms. Molloy stated that her Daughter was asked for the marriage license when she was trying to get a European Union passport, that this is when she realized there was no record, that she remembers going down to the basement of the Church and signing a bunch of documents, and that she did not get a license before that. Ms. Molloy also stated that the original Holy Sacrament of Matrimony has a seal, and that when they went to City Hall and asked for a recording of the license, they were told there was no marriage license. The Court stated that it is possible that they looked for a returned license and not the issued license, that it needs more evidence and that a significant factor is that there was a license issued. The Petition to Establish Fact of Marriage is continued to July 29, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Molloy to go back to the Registrar to see if a license was issued, to go back to the Church to see what records they have of what was signed and for filing of a declaration under penalty of perjury as to what information she obtained and what she recalls of the day of the ceremony. The declaration is to be filed by July 25, 2019.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
